Plaintiff Tammy Traverso'S Motion To Quash Subpoenas Issued By Def. Pma To Richard Traverso, Stephen Griffen, And David Griffen
Off Calendar Dec-27-2021 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: Plaintiff Tammy Traverso's Motion To Quash Subpoenas Issued By Defendant PMA To Richard Traverso, Stephen Griffen, And David Griffen.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Motion is denied. Courts liberally construe the right to discovery. (Williams v. Sup. Ct. (2017) 3 Cal.5th 531, 541.). One seeking to obtain a protective order must show that the burden, expense, or intrusiveness involved "clearly outweighs the likelihood that the information sought will lead to the discovery of admissible evidence." (Emerson Elec. Co. v. Sup. Ct. (1997) 16 Cal.4th 1101, 1110.). Whether something is reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence is liberally construed. (Colonial Life & Accident Ins. Co. v. Sup. Ct. (1982) 31 Cal.3d 785, 790.). The individuals to be deposed are in a position to provide information regarding both liability and damages. Whether plaintiff intends to call either as a witness is not the standard. As to plaintiff's spouse the spousal communication does not apply so as to prevent the deposition as the action is brought for the immediate benefit of the community (Evid. Code Sec. 973; see Hand v. Sup. Ct. (1982) 134 Cal.App.ed 436, 442.) Nothing in this order however, shall prevent plaintiff or the deponent from interposing specific objections to particular questions at the deposition.

The prevailing party is required to prepare and submit a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem at rkane1089@aol.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Robert Kane; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/JPT)
